item.dc:creator: Shannon Pettypiece
item.description: Under President Donald Trump’s “big, beautiful bill,” food stamp applicants are fighting to prove eligibility. A crisis in Arizona offers a warning for America.

media:content.media:text: People wait in line at St. Mary’s Food Bank, Surprise Neighborhood Food Center, Surprise, Ariz., on April 28th, 2026.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Tiffany Hudson and son Jamie Hudson at their residence. Phoenix on April 28th, 2026.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: People wait in line at St. Mary’s Food Bank
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: SNAP in Arizona
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Dawn Savinski, 62, checked in with a volunteer at St. Mary’s Food Bank.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Enoch Asmah, 41, of Surprise, started volunteering at St. Mary’s Food Bank after coming to the U.S. from Ghana.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Arizona SNAP
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: A volunteers at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix directs traffic the morning of April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: There was a steady stream of cars throughout the morning of April 29 at St. Mary's Food Bank in Phoenix.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Jena Hannon was among the more than 1,000 people who came to St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix on April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: The Arizona Department of Economic Security location in Peoria had a line of more than 30 people outside its doors when it opened April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Volunteers loaded food into one of the waiting cars at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix on April 29.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: Volunteers filled grocery carts with food to load into vehicles at St. Mary’s Food Bank in Phoenix.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

media:content.media:text: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.
media:content.media:description: St. Mary’s Food Bank in Surprise, Ariz., drew hundreds of people picking up groceries on April 28.

item.title: Hungry children, canceled benefits: Arizona’s food stamp cuts are a warning for America

item.description: The White House will host the first ever UFC fight on the South Lawn as part of the America's 250th birthday celebrations, setting up a push among MMA fans and MAGA supporters to get tickets. NBC News’ Jonathan Allen reports that President Donald Trump will handpick a lot of the 4,000 attendants, while the public will be able to watch a broadcast of the fight on the Ellipse.

media:content.media:title: The fight is on over tickets to the White House UFC fight
media:content.media:description: The White House will host the first ever UFC fight on the South Lawn as part of the America's 250th birthday celebrations, setting up a push among MMA fans and MAGA supporters to get tickets. NBC News’ Jonathan Allen reports that President Donald Trump will handpick a lot of the 4,000 attendants, while the public will be able to watch a broadcast of the fight on the Ellipse.

item.title: The fight is on over tickets to the White House UFC fight

item.description: Watch live coverage as President Donald Trump holds an event on maternal healthcare at the White House. The administration had announced a proposal to expand workplace fertility benefits and insurance coverage.

media:content.media:title: Trump holds event on maternal healthcare
media:content.media:description: Watch live coverage as President Donald Trump holds an event on maternal healthcare at the White House. The administration had announced a proposal to expand workplace fertility benefits and insurance coverage.

item.title: Trump holds event on maternal healthcare

item.description: Americans from the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak have landed in the United States to be monitored in Nebraska. Officials say one person has tested positive for the Andes strain of the virus and another has mild symptoms. NBC’s Camila Bernal reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Americans From Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Arrive in US for Monitoring
media:content.media:description: Americans from the cruise ship at the center of a hantavirus outbreak have landed in the United States to be monitored in Nebraska. Officials say one person has tested positive for the Andes strain of the virus and another has mild symptoms. NBC’s Camila Bernal reports for TODAY.

item.title: Americans From Hantavirus-Hit Cruise Arrive in US for Monitoring

item.description: President Donald Trump has announced that the United States is rejecting Iran’s latest counter proposal passed on by Pakistan, writing, “I don’t like it– totally unacceptable.” Iran says its demands include the United States lifting its blockade and releasing Iranian frozen assets. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Trump Calls Iran’s Response to US Peace Proposal ‘Unacceptable’
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump has announced that the United States is rejecting Iran’s latest counter proposal passed on by Pakistan, writing, “I don’t like it– totally unacceptable.” Iran says its demands include the United States lifting its blockade and releasing Iranian frozen assets. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

item.title: Trump Calls Iran’s Response to US Peace Proposal ‘Unacceptable’

item.description: President Donald Trump is headed to China for a high-stakes summit with President Xi Jinping where Iran and its impact on the global economy is expected to take center stage. Meanwhile, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy is facing backlash for his latest project, a family road trip reality show that he says was paid for by a nonprofit group instead of taxpayers. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: Trump Heads to China; Sean Duffy Under Fire for Reality Show
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump is headed to China for a high-stakes summit with President Xi Jinping where Iran and its impact on the global economy is expected to take center stage. Meanwhile, Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy is facing backlash for his latest project, a family road trip reality show that he says was paid for by a nonprofit group instead of taxpayers. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

item.title: Trump Heads to China; Sean Duffy Under Fire for Reality Show

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
Gary Grumbach
Fiona Glisson
item.description: The man charged with attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump at the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner last month pleaded not guilty at a Monday arraignment in federal court

media:content.media:text: Cole Allen.
media:content.media:description: Cole Tomas Allen.

item.title: White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty in federal court

item.title: Inside the facility monitoring Americans exposed to hantavirus

item.description: Nearly a year after President Donald Trump signed his “big, beautiful bill” into law, millions are losing SNAP benefits – and Arizona is becoming one of the clearest examples of the fallout. NBC News’ Shannon Pettypiece reports.

media:content.media:title: Arizona food banks swamped by SNAP cuts
media:content.media:description: Nearly a year after President Donald Trump signed his “big, beautiful bill” into law, millions are losing SNAP benefits – and Arizona is becoming one of the clearest examples of the fallout. NBC News’ Shannon Pettypiece reports.

item.title: Arizona food banks swamped by SNAP cuts

item.description: Sen. Cory Booker (D-N.J.) shared his opinion on the U.S. Supreme Court imposing new limits on the Voting Rights Act.

item.description: Matt Damon returns to 'SNL' as Justice Brett Kavanaugh

item.title: Matt Damon returns to 'SNL' as Justice Brett Kavanaugh

item.description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

media:content.media:title: The evolution of album rollouts
media:content.media:description: As album rollouts continue to evolve, fans recently attempted to melt a giant ice block in the middle of Toronto as part of Drake's "Iceman" announcement. But how did we get here? NBC News' Gadi Schwartz takes a look back at other memorable rollouts, and how they have advanced through the years.

item.title: The evolution of album rollouts

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
item.description: Missouri Tigers football star Ahmad Hardy, one of the top running backs in college football, was shot at a concert in Mississippi, the team announced Monday in a statement.

media:content.media:text: Missouri Tigers running back Ahmad Hardy
media:content.media:description: Missouri Tigers running back Ahmad Hardy.

item.title: Missouri running back Ahmad Hardy shot while at a concert in Mississippi

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
Austin Mullen
item.description: Seattle police are looking for a suspect in connection with the Sunday evening murder of a University of Washington student, found inside a school-affiliated apartment building, officials said

media:content.media:text: Nordheim Court, University of Washington campus housing.
media:content.media:description: Nordheim Court, a University of Washington campus housing complex in Seattle.

item.title: Search underway after University of Washington student is fatally stabbed

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
Gary Grumbach

item.title: White House Correspondents’ Dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty in federal court
